hero.headline: UPES Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA with a Human Resource Management specialisation. NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, UGC-DEB approved. Industrial Relations and Labour Legislation, Cross Cultural and Global HRM, Performance Management, Training and Development, OD and Change Management, Digital HR, and Compensation Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: UPES Online MBA HRM: NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, fees Rs 1,75,000 (Year 1: Rs 95,000 / Year 2: Rs 80,000), zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month. HR depth with Industrial Relations, Cross Cultural HRM, Performance Management, T&D, OD, Digital HR, Compensation Management, and Innovation and Entrepreneurship. Rare Digital HR subject in Sem 4.

about.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Human Resource Management is offered by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies, Dehradun. UPES holds NAAC A accreditation, NIRF #45 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #36 in Management (2025). The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The HRM specialisation delivers dedicated Sem 3-4 subjects covering Industrial Relations and Labour Legislation, Cross Cultural and Global HRM, Performance Management, Training and Development, and Organisational Change and Intervention Strategies in Sem 3. Sem 4 covers Strategic Management, Digital HR, Compensation Management, Innovation and Entrepreneurship, and Dissertation.

Digital HR as a dedicated Sem 4 subject is uncommon among Indian online MBA HRM programmes - it covers AI-assisted recruitment tools, HRIS platforms, and HR digital transformation. Cross Cultural and Global HRM in Sem 3 targets HR professionals at MNCs, shared services centres, and global mobility teams. Combined, these subjects give UPES HRM a forward-looking orientation beyond traditional HR management frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labour Legislation
sem3Subjects.description: Apply Industrial Disputes Act, Contract Labour Act, POSH compliance, and collective bargaining for HR compliance and IR management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Cross Cultural and Global HRM
sem3Subjects.description: Design expatriate management policies, global mobility frameworks, and cross-cultural talent strategy for multinational HR roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Performance Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build KPI frameworks, manage appraisal calibration cycles, design continuous feedback systems, and implement performance improvement processes.

sem3Subjects.name: Training and Development
sem3Subjects.description: Design L&D frameworks, build capability programmes, manage LMS platforms, and evaluate training ROI for L&D management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Organisational Change and Intervention Strategies
sem3Subjects.description: Lead OD interventions, manage large-scale culture transformation, and apply change management frameworks to HR-led restructuring.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Align HR strategy with business objectives, develop workforce planning models, and design HR-led organisational transformation programmes.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital HR
sem4Subjects.description: Implement HRIS platforms, manage AI-assisted recruitment tools, and lead digital HR transformation for modern people operations roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Compensation Management
sem4Subjects.description: Design CTC structures, manage pay bands, implement variable pay programmes, and conduct compensation benchmarking for total rewards roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Innovation and Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Apply innovation frameworks to HR product design and build entrepreneurial people management approaches for business growth roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Dissertation
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied HR research project on digital HR transformation, performance management system design, or cross-cultural workforce strategy.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all UPES MBA specialisations. Core includes Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Marketing Management, Quantitative Techniques, Operations Management, OB and HRM, Project Management, Financial Management, Research Methodology, and Business Analytics for Managers.

whoHires.body:
Human Resource Management MBA graduates from UPES Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT services and product companies, BFSI, FMCG, Manufacturing, Energy and petroleum, Consulting

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Executive, L&D Coordinator, HR Generalist, Compensation Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, Compensation Manager, HR Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** HR Director, CHRO, Head of Talent Management, VP HR, Head of People Operations

**Top hiring organisations (UPES alumni placements):** TCS, Infosys, HCL, Wipro, Deloitte, KPMG, and energy sector companies. UPES's industry connect with oil and gas, power, and infrastructure sectors creates direct HR career pathways at companies like ONGC, NTPC, and GAIL that actively recruit UPES MBA graduates.

skills.body:
The UPES HRM specialisation builds skills that IT, manufacturing, and energy sector HR employers test at HRBP and HR manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Industrial Relations and Labour Legislation: Industrial Disputes Act compliance, POSH implementation, Contract Labour Act management, and collective bargaining for HR compliance and IR roles
- Compensation Management: CTC structuring, pay band design, variable pay programme management, and compensation benchmarking for total rewards and compensation roles
- Performance Management: KPI framework design, appraisal calibration, continuous feedback system implementation, and PIP management for HRBP roles
- Cross Cultural and Global HRM: expatriate policy design, global mobility framework management, and cross-cultural talent strategy for MNC HR roles

**Applied skills:**
- Digital HR: HRIS platform implementation, AI-assisted recruitment tool management, and digital HR transformation leadership - a dedicated subject uncommon in peer online MBA HRM programmes
- Organisational Change and Intervention Strategies: OD intervention design, culture transformation leadership, and change management for HR-led restructuring

**Soft skills:** Conducting compensation and performance conversations, presenting workforce analytics to CHRO and board, managing union relations in manufacturing and energy sectors, and leading digital HR platform adoption.

comparisons.body:
Comparing UPES Online MBA HRM against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000 with strong all-India HR brand recognition. UPES HRM at Rs 1,75,000 adds Digital HR as a dedicated Sem 4 subject - a standalone HR technology subject not available in Amity's HRM curriculum. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking provides a strong credibility anchor for energy and manufacturing sector HR roles.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA HRM:** NMIMS carries strong Mumbai corporate HR brand recognition at Rs 1,60,000-1,95,000. For Mumbai BFSI and corporate HR roles, NMIMS carries stronger recognition. UPES HRM includes Digital HR and Cross Cultural HRM as dedicated subjects, making it more relevant for IT services HR and energy sector multinational HR roles.

**vs LPU Online MBA HRM:** LPU is NIRF #31 at Rs 1,61,600 with a dedicated HR Analytics subject. UPES HRM includes Digital HR and Compensation Management as dedicated subjects. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than LPU's management category standing. For energy sector and North India manufacturing HR careers, UPES carries stronger alumni network advantages.

fees.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Human Resource Management has a total program fee of Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years (4 semesters).

**Year-wise fee schedule:**
- Year 1 (Semesters 1 and 2): Rs 95,000
- Year 2 (Semesters 3 and 4): Rs 80,000

**Zero-cost EMI (no-cost EMI available):**
- Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months
- Tenure options: 3 to 36 months
- Zero interest (0% cost EMI) through UPES NBFC lending tie-ups
- Contact our counsellor for current tenure options and approval terms

**Scholarships available (confirmed from UPES scholarships portal):**
- **Academic/Work Experience scholarship**: 10% for 3+ years experience with 55%+ graduation marks; 15% for 5+ years experience; 20% for 10+ years experience
- **Alumni scholarship**: 20% for UPES degree programme graduates
- **Vijay (Sports) scholarship**: 35% to 100% based on national/international achievement level
- Scholarships cannot be combined with corporate MOU discounts
- Scholarship benefit applied semester-wise; eligibility confirmed at admission

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with UPES each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) and carries the UPES name with the MBA designation. The Human Resource Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. UPES is WES recognised, making the degree suitable for international credential evaluation.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from UPES Online MBA Human Resource Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Pooja Rawat
items.liked: Digital HR subject was the standout - directly applicable to my HRIS implementation project at a Dehradun IT company. Industrial Relations gave strong labour law grounding for manufacturing HR.
items.disliked: Placement support was stronger for energy sector companies than for IT services HR roles outside Uttarakhand.
items.body: I had 4 years in HR operations and the UPES HRM MBA helped me move to HRBP. NIRF #36 in Management was a strong credential. Digital HR subject was a genuine differentiator in interviews with tech companies.

items.name: Sanjay Negi
items.liked: Cross Cultural and Global HRM gave strong MNC HR frameworks. Compensation Management subject was directly applicable to my total rewards role.
items.disliked: For pan-India HR careers, Amity or NMIMS carry stronger brand recognition. UPES brand is strongest in North India and energy sectors.
items.body: Good programme for North India IT services and energy sector HR careers. UPES alumni network in Delhi NCR and Dehradun was helpful for lateral HR roles.

items.name: Kavita Sharma
items.liked: OD and Change Management and T&D subjects were well-structured and directly applicable.
items.disliked: LMS platform stability was inconsistent during peak semester periods. Live sessions sometimes had connectivity issues.
items.body: Decent credential for North India HR careers. Best value for energy sector HR professionals targeting ONGC, NTPC, and infrastructure companies where UPES brand is well known.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for UPES Online MBA HRM?
faqs.answer: The total program fee is Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years. Year 1 is Rs 95,000 and Year 2 is Rs 80,000. Zero-cost EMI is available at Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months. Reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before payment.

faqs.question: What scholarships does UPES offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: UPES offers Academic/Work Experience scholarship (10% for 3+ years, 15% for 5+ years, 20% for 10+ years experience with 55%+ graduation), Alumni scholarship (20%), and Vijay Sports scholarship (35% to 100%). Eligibility confirmed at admission.

faqs.question: What is Digital HR subject in UPES HRM MBA?
faqs.answer: Digital HR is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering HRIS platform implementation, AI-assisted recruitment tools, and digital HR transformation. This subject is uncommon as a standalone module in peer online MBA HRM programmes at this fee range and is directly applicable to HR professionals managing HRIS platforms and digital people operations.

faqs.question: Is UPES HRM MBA suitable for energy sector HR roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. UPES's 20+ year legacy in energy, petroleum, and infrastructure education gives HR MBA graduates direct access to alumni networks at ONGC, NTPC, GAIL, and infrastructure companies. Industrial Relations and Labour Legislation is particularly relevant for energy sector HR professionals managing large unionised workforces.

faqs.question: How does UPES HRM compare to LPU HRM in terms of content?
faqs.answer: LPU HRM includes a dedicated HR Analytics subject. UPES HRM includes Digital HR and Compensation Management as dedicated subjects not in LPU's curriculum. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than LPU's management category standing. Both are comparable at similar fee levels with different subject strengths.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A, NIRF #36 Management HRM MBA with Digital HR and Compensation Management at Rs 1,75,000.
You are targeting HRBP, digital HR, or compensation management roles in North India IT services, manufacturing, or energy sector companies.
You want zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month and WES-recognised degree credentials.
consider: If pan-India HR brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA HRM or NMIMS HRM offer stronger national recognition.
If dedicated HR Analytics as a subject is required, LPU Online MBA HRM includes HR Analytics at Rs 1,61,600 with zero-cost EMI.
If AI-driven HR transformation (recruitment automation, predictive analytics) is the focus, JAIN AI for HR specialisation delivers deeper AI-HR curriculum.
